children.label: A
children.text: is capable of absorbing or converting energy, storing the energy for a period of time, and dispatching the energy; and

children.label: i
children.text: uses mechanical, electrochemical, thermal, electrolysis, or other processes to convert and store electric energy that was generated at an earlier time for use at a later time;

children.label: ii
children.text: uses mechanical, electrochemical, biochemical, or thermal processes to convert and store energy generated from mechanical processes that would otherwise be wasted, for delivery at a later time; or

children.label: iii
children.text: stores energy in an electric, thermal, or gaseous state for direct use for heating or cooling at a later time in a manner that avoids the need to use electricity or other fuel sources at that later time, such as a grid-enabled water heater.

children.heading: Program
children.label: 2 Program
children.text: The term “program” means the Energy Storage System Research, Development, and Deployment Program established under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 3 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Energy.

children.heading: Energy Storage System Research, Development, and Deployment Program
children.label: b Energy Storage System Research, Development, and Deployment Program

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall establish a program, to be known as the Energy Storage System Research, Development, and Deployment Program.December 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: for large-scale commercial deployment;

children.label: ii
children.text: for deployment at cost targets established by the Secretary;

children.label: iii
children.text: for hourly and subhourly durations required to provide reliability services to the grid;

children.label: iv
children.text: for daily durations, which have the capacity to discharge energy for a minimum of 6 hours;

children.label: v
children.text: for weekly or monthly durations, which have the capacity to discharge energy for 10 to 100 hours, at a minimum; and

children.label: vi
children.text: for seasonal durations, which have the capability to address seasonal variations in supply and demand;

children.label: B
children.text: distributed energy storage technologies and applications, including building-grid integration;

children.label: C
children.text: long-term cost, performance, and demonstration targets for different types of energy storage systems and for use in a variety of regions, including rural areas;

children.label: D
children.text: transportation energy storage technologies and applications, including vehicle-grid integration;

children.label: i
children.text: the sustainable and secure sourcing, reclamation, recycling, and disposal of energy storage systems, including critical minerals; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the reuse and repurposing of energy storage system technologies;

children.label: F
children.text: advanced control methods for energy storage systems;

children.label: I
children.text: systems with adjustable-speed and other new pumping and generating equipment designs;

children.label: II
children.text: modular systems;

children.label: III
children.text: closed-loop systems, including mines and quarries; and

children.label: IV
children.text: other innovative equipment and materials as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: reductions of civil works costs and construction times for hydropower and pumped storage systems, including comprehensive data and systems analysis of hydropower and pumped storage construction technologies and processes in order to identify areas for whole-system efficiency gains;

children.label: i
children.text: power and water supply systems;

children.label: ii
children.text: electric generation portfolio optimization; and

children.label: iii
children.text: expanded deployment of other renewable energy technologies, including in integrated energy storage systems;

children.label: I
children.text: energy storage use cases from individual and combination technology applications, including value from various-use cases and energy storage services; and

children.label: J
children.text: advanced manufacturing technologies that have the potential to improve United States competitiveness in energy storage manufacturing or reduce United States dependence on critical materials.

children.label: A
children.text: storage technologies, controls, and power electronics for energy storage systems under a variety of operating conditions;

children.label: B
children.text: standardized and grid performance testing for energy storage systems, materials, and technologies during each stage of development;

children.label: C
children.text: reliability, safety, degradation, and durability testing under standard and evolving duty cycles; and

children.label: D
children.text: accelerated life testing protocols to predict estimated lifetime metrics with accuracy.

children.heading: Periodic evaluation of program objectives
children.label: 4 Periodic evaluation of program objectives
children.text: Not less frequently than once every calendar year, the Secretary shall evaluate and, if necessary, update the program objectives to ensure that the program continues to advance energy storage systems toward widespread commercial deployment by lowering the costs and increasing the duration of energy storage resources.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall develop a 10-year strategic plan for the program, and update the plan, in accordance with this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: be coordinated with and integrated across other relevant offices in the Department;

children.label: ii
children.text: to the extent practicable, include metrics that can be used to evaluate storage technologies;

children.label: I
children.text: support the research and development activities described in paragraph (2) and the demonstration projects under subsection (c); and

children.label: aa
children.text: do not support the activities or projects described in subclause (I); but

children.label: bb
children.text: are important to the development of energy storage systems and the mission of the Department, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: I
children.text: the accomplishment of relevant objectives under current programs of the Department relating to energy storage systems; and

children.label: II
children.text: the commencement of any new initiatives within the Department relating to energy storage systems to accomplish those objectives; and

children.label: v
children.text: incorporate relevant activities described in the Grid Modernization Initiative Multi-Year Program Plan.

children.heading: Submission to Congress
children.label: C Submission to Congress
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate and the Committees on Energy and Commerce and Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives the strategic plan developed under subparagraph (A).December 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: shall annually review the strategic plan developed under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: may periodically revise the strategic plan as appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: the Office of Electricity;

children.label: B
children.text: the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy, including the Water Power Technologies Office; and

children.label: i
children.text: the Basic Energy Sciences Program;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Advanced Scientific Computing Research Program;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Biological and Environmental Research Program; and

children.label: D
children.text: the Electricity Storage Research Initiative established under .section 16315 of this title

children.heading: Protecting privacy and security
children.label: 7 Protecting privacy and security
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary shall identify, incorporate, and follow best practices for protecting the privacy of individuals and businesses and the respective sensitive data of the individuals and businesses, including by managing privacy risk and implementing the Fair Information Practice Principles of the Federal Trade Commission for the collection, use, disclosure, and retention of individual electric consumer information in accordance with the Office of Management and Budget Circular A–130 (or successor circulars).

children.heading: Energy storage demonstration projects; pilot grant program
children.label: c Energy storage demonstration projects; pilot grant program
A
children.text: at least 1 energy storage system demonstration project designed to further the development of technologies described in clause (v) or (vi) of subsection (b)(2)(A); and

children.label: B
children.text: 1 project to demonstrate second-life applications of electric vehicle batteries as aggregated energy storage installations to provide services to the electric grid, in accordance with paragraph (3).

children.label: i
children.text: a State energy office (as defined in );section 15821(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: an Indian Tribe (as defined in ;section 4103 of title 251So in original. A closing parenthesis probably should precede the semicolon.1

children.label: iii
children.text: a Tribal organization (as defined in );section 3765 of title 38

children.label: iv
children.text: an institution of higher education (as defined in );section 1001 of title 20

children.label: I
children.text: an electric cooperative;

children.label: II
children.text: a political subdivision of a State, such as a municipally owned electric utility, or any agency, authority, corporation, or instrumentality of a State political subdivision; and

children.label: III
children.text: an investor-owned utility; and

children.label: vi
children.text: a private energy storage company.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: B Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a competitive grant program under which the Secretary shall award grants to eligible entities to carry out demonstration projects for pilot energy storage systems.

children.label: i
children.text: ensure regional diversity among eligible entities awarded grants, including ensuring participation of eligible entities that are rural States and States with high energy costs;

children.label: I
children.text: expand on the existing technology demonstration programs of the Department;

children.label: II
children.text: are designed to achieve 1 or more of the objectives described in subparagraph (D); and

children.label: III
children.text: inject or withdraw energy from the bulk power system, electric distribution system, building energy system, or microgrid (grid-connected or islanded mode) where the project is located;

children.label: iii
children.text: give consideration to proposals from eligible entities for securing energy storage through competitive procurement or contract for service; and

children.label: iv
children.text: prioritize projects that leverage matching funds from non-Federal sources.

children.label: i
children.text: To improve the security of critical infrastructure and emergency response systems.

children.label: ii
children.text: To improve the reliability of transmission and distribution systems, particularly in rural areas, including high-energy cost rural areas.

children.label: iii
children.text: To optimize transmission or distribution system operation and power quality to defer or avoid costs of replacing or upgrading electric grid infrastructure, including transformers and substations.

children.label: iv
children.text: To supply energy at peak periods of demand on the electric grid or during periods of significant variation of electric grid supply.

children.label: v
children.text: To reduce peak loads of homes and businesses.

children.label: vi
children.text: To improve and advance power conversion systems.

children.label: vii
children.text: To provide ancillary services for grid stability and management.

children.label: viii
children.text: To integrate renewable energy resource production.

children.label: ix
children.text: To increase the feasibility of microgrids (grid-connected or islanded mode).

children.label: x
children.text: To enable the use of stored energy in forms other than electricity to support the natural gas system and other industrial processes.

children.label: xi
children.text: To integrate fast charging of electric vehicles.

children.label: xii
children.text: To improve energy efficiency.

children.heading: Demonstration of electric vehicle battery second-life applications for grid services
children.label: 3 Demonstration of electric vehicle battery second-life applications for grid services

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall enter into an agreement to carry out a project to demonstrate second-life applications of electric vehicle batteries as aggregated energy storage installations to provide services to the electric grid.

children.label: i
children.text: to demonstrate power safety and the reliability of the applications demonstrated under the program;

children.label: I
children.text: to provide ancillary services for grid stability and management; and

children.label: II
children.text: to reduce the peak loads of homes and businesses;

children.label: iii
children.text: to extend the useful life of electric vehicle batteries and the components of electric vehicle batteries prior to the collection, recycling, and reprocessing of the batteries and components; and

children.label: iv
children.text: to increase acceptance of, and participation in, the use of second-life applications of electric vehicle batteries by utilities.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: C Priority
children.text: In selecting a project to carry out under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall give priority to projects in which the demonstration of the applicable second-life applications is paired with 1 or more facilities that could particularly benefit from increased resiliency and lower energy costs, such as a multi-family affordable housing facility, a senior care facility, and a community health center.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 4 Reports
children.text: Not less frequently than once every 3 years for the duration of the programs under paragraphs (1) and (2), the Secretary shall submit to Congress and make publicly available a report describing the performance of those programs.

children.heading: No project ownership interest
children.label: 5 No project ownership interest
children.text: The Federal Government shall not hold any equity or other ownership interest in any energy storage system that is part of a project under this subsection unless the holding is agreed to by each participant of the project.

children.heading: Long-duration demonstration initiative and joint program
children.label: d Long-duration demonstration initiative and joint program

children.heading: Initiative
children.label: A Initiative
children.text: The term “Initiative” means the demonstration initiative established under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Joint Program
children.label: B Joint Program
children.text: The term “Joint Program” means the joint program established under paragraph (4).

children.heading: Establishment of Initiative
children.label: 2 Establishment of Initiative
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall establish a demonstration initiative composed of demonstration projects focused on the development of long-duration energy storage technologies.December 27, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: ensure a range of technology types;

children.label: B
children.text: ensure regional diversity among projects; and

children.label: C
children.text: consider bulk power level, distribution power level, behind-the-meter, microgrid (gridconnected or islanded mode), and off-grid applications.

children.label: i
children.text: to demonstrate promising long-duration energy storage technologies at different scales; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to help new, innovative long-duration energy storage technologies become commercially viable.

children.heading: Memorandum of understanding
children.label: B Memorandum of understanding
children.text: Not later than 200 days after , the Secretary shall enter into a memorandum of understanding with the Secretary of Defense to administer the Joint Program.December 27, 2020

children.label: I
children.text: Department facilities; and

children.label: II
children.text: Department of Defense installations; and

children.label: ii
children.text: develop new infrastructure for identified projects, if appropriate.

children.heading: Goals and metrics
children.label: D Goals and metrics
children.text: The Secretary and the Secretary of Defense shall develop goals and metrics for technological progress under the Joint Program consistent with energy resilience and energy security policies.

children.label: I
children.text: ensure that projects are carried out under conditions that represent a variety of environments with different physical conditions and market constraints; and

children.label: aa
children.text: larger, higher-cost projects; and

children.label: bb
children.text: smaller, lower-cost projects.

children.label: I
children.text: make available to the public project information that will accelerate deployment of long-duration energy storage technologies; and

children.label: II
children.text: will be carried out in the field.

children.heading: Pumped storage hydropower wind and solar integration and system reliability initiative
children.label: e Pumped storage hydropower wind and solar integration and system reliability initiative
I
children.text: a political subdivision of a State, such as a municipally owned electric utility; or

children.label: II
children.text: an instrumentality of a State composed of municipally owned electric utilities;

children.label: ii
children.text: an electric cooperative; or

children.label: iii
children.text: an investor-owned utility;

children.label: B
children.text: an Indian Tribe or Tribal organization;

children.label: C
children.text: a State energy office;

children.label: D
children.text: an institution of higher education; and

children.label: E
children.text: a consortium of the entities described in subparagraphs (A) through (D).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall, to the maximum extent practicable, enter into an agreement with an eligible entity to provide financial assistance to the eligible entity to carry out project design, transmission studies, power market assessments, and permitting for a pumped storage hydropower project to facilitate the long-duration storage of intermittent renewable electricity.September 30, 2023

children.label: i
children.text: be designed to provide not less than 1,000 megawatts of storage capacity;

children.label: ii
children.text: be able to provide energy and capacity for use in more than 1 organized electricity market;

children.label: iii
children.text: be able to store electricity generated by intermittent renewable electricity projects located on Tribal land; and

children.label: iv
children.text: have received a preliminary permit from the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission.

children.heading: Matching requirement
children.label: C Matching requirement
children.text: An eligible entity receiving financial assistance under subparagraph (A) shall provide matching funds equal to or greater than the amount of financial assistance provided under that subparagraph.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.
to ensure appropriate collaboration;
to avoid unnecessary duplication of those activities; and
to increase domestic manufacturing and production of energy storage systems, such as those within the Department and within the National Institute of Standards and Technology.
to carry out subsection (b), $100,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025, to remain available until expended;
to carry out subsection (c), $71,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025, to remain available until expended; and
to carry out subsection (d), $30,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025, to remain available until expended.
